game_description: Claim railway routes across North America, 2-5 players.

strategies.strategy_detailed: Rule: you must keep at least 2 of your 3 initial tickets. Pick tickets that share a geographic cluster — overlapping routes mean fewer cards complete multiple tickets simultaneously. Discard outliers when allowed; the penalty for failing each ticket = its full point value.
Long-distance tickets (10+ points) are high-risk, high-reward. Keep one big ticket paired with two shorter ones. Completing the long route also builds the path for the short ones.
strategy_stealth: Long route builds short route path

strategies.condition: Wild card visible at game start
strategies.strategy_detailed: Face-up locomotives cost your entire turn — you take 1 card instead of 2. The wild's value is flexibility: required for gray routes, usable for any color. Worth it turn 1 if no urgent route is contested. Skip if a 4-5 car route needs claiming before opponents draw the same colors.
If an opponent is collecting cards of the color you need for a critical route, claim that route immediately even if you planned to wait. Losing a bottleneck route can invalidate multiple tickets.
When two routes connect the same cities, claim the one that also extends toward your next ticket destination. Efficiency compounds: each route should serve at least two purposes.

Take face-up cards only when they match colors you need. If two different needed colors appear face-up, take both. Don't grab useless face-ups — blind draw has ~10% wild chance and usually beats a junk colored card. Refresh: 3 visible wilds reshuffle all 5 face-ups.

Plan a connected spine through your ticket network. Longest Train is 10 bonus points — worth more than most individual tickets. Extend routes at every opportunity rather than building isolated segments.
Skip routes that don't directly complete tickets. Short efficient paths to ticket completions beat a long sprawling network. Draw new tickets at mid-game once initial set is nearly done.

strategies.condition: Opponent clearly needs a specific route
strategies.strategy_detailed: Block opponent's critical bottleneck routes when the block costs you little (gray routes, short segments). Blocking is rarely worth building a whole detour, but snapping up a contested 2-3 car route is often correct.
Claim the route immediately. Holding large color sets invites discard if you draw tickets you don't need or if the game ends faster than expected. Cards in hand are not points.
Draw new tickets only when your primary network is 70%+ complete. New tickets that share cities with existing routes are worth keeping. Discard tickets that would require a wholly new network branch.
When another player is within 3-4 cars of your current length, extend aggressively. The Longest Train can only be claimed by one player. A 10-point swing is often game-deciding.

Secure your last route immediately even if adjacent contested ones cost more. Failing a ticket subtracts its full point value AND denies the gain you would have earned — net swing is 2× the ticket value vs completing. Blocked final segments are catastrophic.

strategies.condition: Deciding when to trigger end game
strategies.strategy_detailed: Trigger end game (go to 2 or 0 trains) only when all your tickets are complete or you cannot complete remaining tickets regardless. Triggering early robs opponents of turns but also limits your own options.

strategies.condition: Opponent is about to trigger end game
strategies.strategy_detailed: When an opponent has 2 or fewer trains left, you get one more turn after they place their last train. Prioritize completing your highest-value incomplete ticket this turn — even a partial completion may not be worth it.

strategies.condition: Longest Train race in final turns
strategies.strategy_detailed: If Longest Train is contested in the final turns, calculate exact car counts before committing. One opponent's double route can overtake your length instantly. Having 2-3 extra cars of buffer is the minimum safe margin.

If a ticket is still incomplete in the last 2 turns, evaluate whether completion is possible. If not, the penalty subtracted is fixed — maximize other sources of points (route lengths, other completed tickets) instead of chasing a doomed ticket.
